claim_raw: _mark_auto_resolved() unconditionally clears suppressed/suppressed_at/suppression_pattern (merge_issues.py:51-55) for every issue it touches, including issues that were explicitly suppressed via .sloppyignore. auto_resolve_disappeared() has no guard to skip suppressed issues before calling _mark_auto_resolved(), so a suppressed open issue that falls outside the current scan_path (or genuinely disappears) loses all suppression metadata. The loss is permanent.
claim_distilled: _mark_auto_resolved() clears suppression metadata unconditionally; auto_resolve_disappeared() has no guard to skip suppressed issues before calling it.
claim_restated: auto_resolve_disappeared() iterates all open/wontfix/fixed/false_positive issues absent from the current scan. For each such issue it calls _mark_auto_resolved(), which sets suppressed=False, suppressed_at=None, suppression_pattern=None unconditionally (merge_issues.py:51-55). There is no `if previous.get('suppressed'): continue` guard anywhere in auto_resolve_disappeared(). A suppressed issue (suppressed=True, status='open') that is either out of the narrow scan_path or has genuinely disappeared will have all three suppression fields cleared. The 'permanent' characterization is inaccurate: on the next in-scope scan upsert_issues() re-evaluates matched_ignore_pattern() and can re-apply suppression if the pattern still matches — but until that broader scan runs, suppression is lost.

claude.reasoning: Core claim independently confirmed. merge_issues.py:51-55 (pre-fix) unconditionally wrote suppressed=False, suppressed_at=None, suppression_pattern=None inside _mark_auto_resolved(). auto_resolve_disappeared() had no suppression guard — verified by absence of any `if previous.get('suppressed'): continue` before either the out-of-scope path (old lines 111-113) or the normal disappear path (old lines 122-131). Caveat: 'permanent' is overstated. On the next broad scan, upsert_issues() calls matched_ignore_pattern() and re-suppresses if the pattern still exists in .sloppyignore — so loss is temporary between the narrow-scope scan and the next full scan. Additional secondary consequence not raised in submission: when a previously-suppressed+auto-resolved issue reappears and the pattern still matches, upsert_issues() sets suppressed=True but hits `continue` before the status reopen check, leaving {status: 'auto_resolved', suppressed: True} — a mixed state hidden by match_issues() but semantically wrong. Fix: add `if previous.get('suppressed'): continue` after the suspect-detector guard, before the scope filter. This is present in current code at merge_issues.py:104-105.

notes: The core claim is valid: _mark_auto_resolved() has no suppression guard and clears suppression metadata on all issues it touches, including explicitly suppressed ones. The missing guard is a one-line fix. The 'permanent' characterization is inaccurate (suppression is recovered on the next in-scope scan), and the submission did not identify the secondary inconsistent-state consequence ({status: 'auto_resolved', suppressed: True}). No scoring engine impact — suppression state is orthogonal to score computation. Fix already landed in main; no code changes needed in this PR.
